creator: marklmi26-fbsd@yahoo.com
text:
(In reply to John F. Carr from comment #0)

My understanding is that FreeBSD tracks Linux *.dts* updates instead of
maintaining its own. When new Linux *.dts* are imported, the blaim for
failures to work go on the FreeBSD kernel and such not correctly being
updated to handle the new structures in the *.dts* files: mixes of
differing vintages of *.dts* materials are never counted as the solution
to keep the FreeBSD kernel (and the like?) as unchanged. (U-Boot could
have tracking issues at times as well.)

So I expect that this comes down to: No one has been maintaining various
BPI-M3 related parts of FreeBSD. That likely means that no volunteer has
had an interest in doing so in some time, based on your 13-and-later
reporting for the problems.  The BPI-M3 need not be the only thing no
longer working because of some of the same *.dts* file changes over the
various imports.
